document: Introduction to Internet of Things Assignment-Week 8 Solutions

questions.questionText: Which architecture proposes the use of an agent placed at access points to communicate with the controller in Software Defined Mobile Networks? [cite: 1214]
questions.explanation: Odin basically proposes the use of an agent-based solution where an agent is placed at the access points to communicate with the controller. [cite: 1220, 1221]

questions.questionText: Which solution was proposed by the SWAN group for mobility-aware flow rule placement in mobile-based IoT systems? [cite: 1223]
questions.explanation: The MobileFlow solution was proposed by the SWAN group and provides a mobility-aware flow rule placement for mobile-based IoT systems. [cite: 1229, 1233]

questions.questionText: In the context of Data Center Networking using SDN, which type of flow requires exact match rules? [cite: 1235]
questions.explanation: For Elephant flows (large-scale flows), it is required to have an exact match of the rules, whereas Mice flows can use wildcard rules. [cite: 1241, 1242]

questions.questionText: Cloud computing is best described as using computing as a: [cite: 1246]
questions.explanation: Cloud computing is all about using computing as a utility, similar to how we use electricity or water. [cite: 1252, 1253]

questions.questionText: Which of the following is NOT an essential characteristic of Cloud Computing? [cite: 1255]
questions.explanation: The essential characteristics include Rapid Elasticity, Measured Service, Resource Pooling, On-demand self-service, and Broad network access. Fixed resource allocation is contrary to elasticity. [cite: 1261, 1262]

questions.questionText: Which deployment model is restricted to a single organization where cloud functionalities are available only within that organization? [cite: 1266]
questions.explanation: Private cloud is typically restricted to a single organization, and the cloud functionalities are made available only within the organization. [cite: 1272, 1273]

questions.questionText: In which service model are the Applications and Data managed by the user, while the Runtime, Middleware, and OS are managed by the provider? [cite: 1275]
questions.explanation: In Platform as a Service (PaaS), only Applications and Data are taken care of by the user; the rest are managed by the service provider. [cite: 1283, 1284]

questions.questionText: Which service model allows customers to rent virtualized servers and associated services, focusing on infrastructure like networking and storage? [cite: 1286]
questions.explanation: Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) allows customers to rent virtualized servers, software, data center space, or network equipment. [cite: 1292, 1293]

questions.questionText: Which of the following is a limitation of the SaaS model? [cite: 1297]
options: c. Data security and privacy concerns [cite: 1300]
d. High switching costs for hardware [cite: 1301]
questions.explanation: The limitations of SaaS include limited flexibility and data security and privacy concerns. [cite: 1303]

questions.questionText: What does SLA stand for in the context of Cloud Computing? [cite: 1305]
questions.explanation: SLA stands for Service Level Agreement, which describes the quality, utility, and warranty of services expected by the customer. [cite: 1313, 1314]

questions.questionText: The "Bluepill" attack is a specific type of threat targeting which component of the cloud infrastructure? [cite: 1316]
questions.explanation: The Bluepill attack is a very popular form of attack on the hypervisor. [cite: 1322]

questions.questionText: Which term refers to the independent expectancy between two entities for a specific context at a given time? [cite: 1326]
questions.explanation: Trust basically takes care of the issue of independent expectancy between two entities for any specific context at a given time. [cite: 1332, 1333]

questions.questionText: Which of the following is a feature of the GreenCloud simulator? [cite: 1335]
options: a. It allows monitoring of energy consumption of networks and devices [cite: 1337]
b. It is a closed-source paid software [cite: 1339]
c. It is developed by Microsoft [cite: 1341]
d. It does not support GUI [cite: 1343]
questions.explanation: GreenCloud offers facilities for monitoring energy consumption of networks and devices and is energy-aware. [cite: 1345, 1346]

questions.questionText: What is the primary difference between Microsoft Azure's PaaS (Platform as a Service) and IaaS (Infrastructure as a Service) offerings? [cite: 1350]
options: a. PaaS is free, IaaS is paid [cite: 1353]
b. PaaS provides a development environment for clients to build apps, while IaaS offers control over OS and infrastructure [cite: 1354]
c. IaaS is for mobile only, PaaS is for web only [cite: 1356]
questions.explanation: PaaS focuses on application development platforms, while IaaS offers total control of the operating system and application stack. [cite: 1360, 1361]

questions.questionText: Which of the following is a Java-based cloud simulation platform developed at the University of Melbourne? [cite: 1363]
questions.explanation: CloudSim is a Java-based simulation platform developed at the University of Melbourne by the team led by Professor Rajkumar Buyya. [cite: 1373, 1374]
